design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character, progress through urban stages, defeat waves of enemies and bosses, rescue city from crime syndicate
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Action-oriented combat against increasing enemy density and boss encounters in side-scrolling levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Ex-police vigilante reclaiming a corrupted metropolis through direct combat with organized crime
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Focused on responsive beat 'em up mechanics with a Shinobi-style screen-clearing special attack; emphasizes immediate, kinetic action over strategic positioning

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with game over on exhaustion
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present (standard for arcade-adjacent genre)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; levels progress linearly with bosses marking stage transitions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection affects playstyle; special attack mechanic provides temporary relief from pressure

progression.unlock_mechanism: Character roster available from start; progression locked behind stage completion
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters (Axel Stone, Blaze Fielding, Adam Hunter) with distinct movesets
Score-based ranking system encourages replays for higher performance

world_building_techniques: Setting: fictional American metropolis Wood Oak City with crime syndicate corruption
Character archetypes: ex-police officers as protagonists establishing authority context
Implicit narrative: character selection and stage progression suggest coordinated vigilante operation

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-culminating encounters with thematic crime syndicate enforcers; Mr. X as final antagonist representing crime lord hierarchy
notable_bosses: Mr. X (final boss, crime syndicate leader)

faction_indicators: Ex-police officers as protagonists (institutional authority faction)
Crime syndicate as antagonist (organized crime faction)
Wood Oak City government implied as corrupted (institutional corruption)
danger_signals: Crime lord control of city government (systemic corruption)
Need for extra-legal vigilante action (authority failure)
Organized enemy waves suggesting hierarchical crime structure
environmental_storytelling: Urban setting with crime-afflicted streets as core play environment
Stage progression through city zones implies territorial gang control
Multiplayer co-op mechanic reinforces teamwork narrative of allied vigilantes
